rows.claim_text: 'The Communities We Serve - Click on a state below to learn more. Currently Serving / Coming Soon' ... state panels: Missouri ('Proudly Serving St. Louis and the Kansas City Metro Area'), Iowa ('Proudly Serving Iowa City, Sioux City, and the Des Moines Metro Areas.'), Nebraska ('Proudly Serving Omaha Metro Area since 1987.'), Wisconsin ('...Metro Milwaukee and Madison Areas since 1973'), Ohio ('...Dayton, Cincinnati, Columbus, and Cleveland Metro Areas.'), Virginia, Massachusetts, Rhode Island, Washington DC, Kansas ('Proudly Serving Kansas and the Kansas City Metro area'), North Carolina ('...Elizabeth City Metro Area'). Coming Soon: Connecticut, South Carolina, Illinois, Indiana, Colorado, Florida.
rows.note: n_states_claimed counts DC as one of 11 'Currently Serving' entries (10 states + DC). Six additional states listed as 'MMT is Coming Soon!'. Also on page: 'In 2022, MMT's fleet traveled more than 9,000,000 miles'; 'MMT began in 1987 in rural Nebraska operating one ambulance and has since become a national industry leader and one of the largest EMS (Emergency Medical Services) companies of its kind.'

rows.claim_text: With a growing fleet of 500+ vehicles and a workforce of 4,000+ providers, MMT strives to provide the absolute best patient care 24/7/365.

MMT has operations spread out across multiple states and offers premier, reliable ambulance transportation services to dozens of communities and thousands of medical centers and healthcare organizations.
MMT began in 1987 in rural Nebraska operating one ambulance and has since become a national industry leader and one of the largest EMS (Emergency Medical Services) companies of its kind.
rows.note: The state-by-state 'Communities We Serve / Currently Serving' section present in the 2023-03 capture is ABSENT from this capture; no state enumeration on page.

rows.claim_text: MMT has operations spread out across multiple states and offers premier, reliable ambulance transportation services to dozens of communities and thousands of medical centers and healthcare organizations.
For over 35 years, MMT has partnered with some of the largest and most prestigious health systems across the country to provide patient ambulance transportation solutions to improve throughput.
rows.note: Redesigned homepage; no state enumeration. State names appear only inside a customer-style testimonial referencing 'the Missouri Valley, Iowa station'.

rows.claim_text: Our team of more than 2,800+ dedicated emergency medicine professionals is committed to providing optimal care to each patient and exceptional service to the health system partners within our communities.

Our team of more than 2,800+ dedicated emergency medicine professionals is committed to providing optimal care to each patient and exceptional service to the health system partners within our communities.

Patient Transport Programs that Improve Health System Throughput - For over 35 years, MMT has partnered with some of the largest and most prestigious health systems across the country to provide patient ambulance transportation solutions to improve throughput.
(Iowa-specific careers page present; page text includes '...MMT creates a fun, rewarding, growth-oriented culture for our 2,800+ team members'.)
rows.note: Existence of a state-specific careers page recorded as-is; not a footprint enumeration.

format_notes: Domain history: midwestmedicaltransport.com was the public site through at least Dec 2014 (Wayback) and still served robots.txt to Common Crawl through 2022; mmtamb.com (MMT Ambulance rebrand) appears in archives from Mar 2023. No archive captures exist for midwestmedtrans.com. Wayback snapshot CONTENT could not be fetched from this environment (egress block) - Wayback rows are recorded with URL + timestamp and PARKED for content; content rows are Common Crawl WARC captures, an independent public web archive, labeled per row. 2015-2022 content: no non-robots captures found in either reachable archive for the old domain; gap is recorded, not interpolated. Self-claims by the subject company on its own public website, recorded verbatim; labeled PUBLIC-WEB self-claim. No inference beyond page text.
